_publications._publicationTitle: Three-Dimensional Spheroid Drug Sensitivity Screening for Neurofibromatosis Type 1-Associated High-Grade Glioma
_publications._fundingAgency: Gilbert Family Foundation; Children's Tumor Foundation; Department of Defense CDMRP; Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation; NHGRI; NINDS; CHOP
_publications._context: we describe a spheroid-based drug combination screen using ultra-low attachment 96-well plates. Spheroid formation occurs overnight, drug combinations are applied, and RFP fluorescence is tracked via Incucyte over 72h to quantify growth and cell death (IC50, LD50)

_publications._publicationTitle: Identification of therapeutic sensitivities in a spheroid drug combination screen of Neurofibromatosis Type I associated High Grade Gliomas
_publications._fundingAgency: Gilbert Family Foundation (722002); Children's Tumor Foundation (CTF-2022-05-002); NHGRI (T32HG009495); NINDS (2R25NS065745-06); Department of Defense CDMRP (W81XWH2010124); Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation; CHOP startup funds
_publications._context: Two thousand cells/well of 17, 5653, or 5746 glioma cell lines are plated in 96-well round-bottomed ultra-low attachment plates and used for spheroid drug screening

basicInfo.description: Scaffold-free ultra-low attachment 96-well spheroid culture system for NF1-associated high grade glioma cell lines; used for combination drug sensitivity screening with Incucyte RFP-based growth and cell death readouts
basicInfo.synonyms: spheroid culture system; NF1 glioma spheroids
basicInfo.modelLimitations: Human cell line (TM-31) showed only additive or antagonistic drug responses in contrast to mouse lines; scaffold-free system may not capture ECM-mediated drug resistance
basicInfo.developerAffiliation: Department of Pediatrics, Children's Hospital Philadelphia; School of Medicine, University of Pennsylvania
